NASA-STD-8739-3-2.pdf - Free download as PDF File (.pdf), Text File (.txt) or read online for free. This document provides standards for soldered electrical connections for space flight hardware and critical ground support equipment. It establishes requirements for facilities, equipment, materials, processes and personnel training.
This document provides standards for soldered electrical connections for space flight hardware and critical ground support equipment. It establishes requirements for facilities, equipment, materials, processes and personnel training. Specific standards are defined for cleanliness, environmental control, tooling, soldering techniques, inspection and certification of personnel.
NASA-STD-8739.3 December 1997 FOREWORD Effective Date: December 15 1997 This Standard provides a baseline for NASA project offices to use when preparing or evaluating process procedures for the manufacture of space flight hardware or mission critical ground support equipment.

results.title: NASA-STD-8739.3 - NMTTC East - NASA's East Coast Workmanship Training Center
results.description: NASA-STD-8739.3 – Soldered Electrical ... This course provides instruction to operators and inspectors who are responsible for using hand soldering operations on printed wiring assemblies or spaceflight hardware....
results.content:
NASA-STD-8739.3 – Soldered Electrical ... This course provides instruction to operators and inspectors who are responsible for using hand soldering operations on printed wiring assemblies or spaceflight hardware....
NASA-STD-8739.3 – Soldered Electrical Connections is NASA’s workmanship standard for hand-soldered PWAs, soldered electrical assemblies, and critical ground-support equipment. This course provides instruction to operators and inspectors who are responsible for using hand soldering operations on printed wiring assemblies or spaceflight hardware.
Inspector – This level of training is applicable only to those who will be performing inspections of printed wiring assemblies and soldered connections fabricated to the NASA-STD-8739.3. Operator – This level of training is applicable to fabricators who will be performing hand soldering operations to printed wiring assemblies or other spaceflight hardware. Operator/Inspector Dual Cert – This training level applies to students who require the flexibility to fabricate spaceflight hardware as well as perform inspections on other operator’s workmanship.
Supplier Level B Instructor – The responsibility of the Level B Instructor is to facilitate training courses to his or her internal team for the NASA workmanship standards. After initial training, the student must return to NMTTC for Level B Instructor training at a 3-day Supplier Level B Instructor course to complete the prerequisite for Supplier Level B Instructor certification. ... Implementation Requirements Inspectors, Operators, and Instructors are bound to the requirements of the NASA-STD-8739.6.
All of the requirements contained in the standard are discussed. This course is a prerequisite for NASA-STD-8739.3 operator, inspector, or level B instructor certification. Vision screening is a prerequisite per NASA-STD-8739.6. Prior hand soldering experience is strongly recommended.

This course provides instruction to operators and inspectors who are responsible for using hand soldering operations on printed wiring assemblies or spaceflight hardware.

Inspector – This level of training is applicable only to those who will be performing inspections of printed wiring assemblies and soldered connections fabricated to the NASA-STD-8739.3. Operator – This level of training is applicable to fabricators who will be performing hand soldering operations to printed wiring assemblies or other spaceflight hardware. Operator/Inspector Dual Cert – This training level applies to students who require the flexibility to fabricate spaceflight hardware as well as perform inspections on other operator’s workmanship.
